definition.name: real_vs_complex_SDP
definition.definition: A critical distinction throughout this work. Real SDP operates over S^n (real symmetric matrices, dim = n(n+1)/2) with PSD cone S^n_+. A rank-r matrix generates a face of dimension r(r+1)/2 in S^n_+, yielding the Barvinok-Pataki bound r(r+1)/2 ≤ m. Complex SDP operates over H^n (Hermitian matrices, dim = n²) with PSD cone H^n_+. A rank-r matrix generates a face of dimension r² in H^n_+, yielding r² ≤ m. Our abstract theorem, working with *-algebras over C, naturally produces the complex version: dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) = Σ n_j², giving Σ n_j² ≤ m+1. The +1 vs. the classical formulation arises because the state space S(A) incorporates normalisation (φ(1)=1) as a built-in constraint, while classical SDP counts the trace constraint among the m affine constraints. For *-algebras with trivial involution (real algebras), one obtains the real version with the symmetric face dimension formula.
